_meta.description: Canada Summer Jobs — federal wage subsidy for hiring youth (15-30) during summer. Relevant for businesses planning to hire a student assistant.

plain_language_summary: Canada Summer Jobs provides a wage subsidy to help businesses and non-profits hire young people aged 15-30 during summer (typically May–August). You hire a student, the government reimburses 50-100% of their minimum wage. Great for micro-businesses that need seasonal help but can't afford a full salary.

amount.for_profit_businesses: 50% reimbursement of provincial minimum wage per hour worked
amount.example: If you hire a student for 35 hours/week for 10 weeks: ~$5,635 total cost. Government reimburses ~$2,817 (50% of minimum wage). Your net cost: ~$2,817 — plus any wages above minimum if you pay more.
amount.note: Subsidy is based on minimum wage even if you pay the student more

employer.must_be: Canadian business with a valid CRA Business Number

immigration_status_employer.note: Business owner must have valid authorization to hire in Canada

employee_youth.age: 15 to 30 years old at start of employment
employee_youth.must_be: Canadian citizen, permanent resident, or person to whom refugee protection has been conferred
employee_youth.note: The YOUTH must meet these criteria — not the business owner

job_requirements.must_be: Full-time position (30-40 hours/week) during summer months
job_requirements.must_not_be: A position that replaces a laid-off worker

application_timeline.when_to_apply: Applications typically open in November–January for the following summer
application_timeline.important: You must apply months in advance — this is NOT a program you can access last-minute
application_timeline.hackathon_note: For the April 2026 hackathon demo, the 2026 summer jobs cycle is already in progress — relevant as a future planning tool for users

how_to_apply: Through the Grants and Contributions Online Services (GCOS) portal at canada.ca

persona_fit.Yara: Relevant if she plans to hire a student helper for her bakery during summer markets
persona_fit.Marcus: Could hire a junior developer or student intern
persona_fit.Fatima: Could hire a student assistant for her daycare (if she has space and meets capacity rules)

common_mistakes: Missing the application window — you must apply in November-January, not in spring
Thinking it covers 100% of wages for for-profit businesses — it only covers 50% of minimum wage
Not accounting for employer contributions (QPP, QPIP, EI) on top of wages — those are your cost
Hiring youth who don't meet the citizenship/PR requirement — the youth themselves must be eligible
